file_insights.changes: Added constant-time token comparison for authentication
file_insights.context: HTTP server endpoint (POST /cards/new) with Bearer token authentication

patterns_discovered.purpose: Mitigate timing attacks on token validation
key_steps: Convert both strings to byte slices with .as_bytes()

gotchas_discovered.issue: Timing attack vulnerability in naive string comparison
gotchas_discovered.risk: Early exit on first mismatched character leaks token length/content info
gotchas_discovered.detection: Simple string equality with early termination

gotchas_discovered.issue: Subtle crate required for secure comparison
gotchas_discovered.detail: Standard Rust == operator is not timing-safe for cryptographic values
gotchas_discovered.requires: Must import and use ConstantTimeEq trait

gotchas_discovered.detail: .unwrap_u8() returns u8 (1 for equal, 0 for not), must compare against 1 explicitly

approach_outcome.key_achievement: Eliminated timing attack vulnerability in token validation
approach_outcome.security_improvement: Replaced timing-vulnerable string comparison with constant-time operation
approach_outcome.code_quality: Added explanatory comment for security reasoning

recommendations.action: Add integration tests for constant-time comparison behavior
recommendations.detail: Verify invalid tokens are rejected with same response time profile as valid tokens

recommendations.action: Audit other authentication/crypto operations in codebase
recommendations.detail: Search for similar timing vulnerabilities in token, password, or credential handling
Add security.md or code comments explaining why constant-time comparison is required
Verify token expiration, rate limiting, and other auth protections are in place
